message:
test(readme): integration test for Reshaping section (fd90.193 / br-tc3g2)

Adds readme_reshaping_compiles_and_runs covering 7 reshaping APIs
documented in README lines 498-524 that previously had no integration
coverage (pivot_table is exercised separately by readme_pivot_tables):

- melt (wide → long with id_vars + value_vars + custom var/value names)
- stack / unstack (column index ↔ row index round-trip on numeric DF)
- crosstab (contingency table from two categorical Series — verifies
  3 unique departments × 2 unique genders dimension contract)
- crosstab_normalize (with "all" normalization mode)
- get_dummies (one-hot encoding across multiple columns)
- xs (cross-section by IndexLabel; uses From<&str> coercion via .into())

Found via self-review on the last 3 commits — all 7 methods exist in
fp-frame but had drifted out of integration coverage.

Verified: 32 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Scope (one new test):
- DataFrame.melt(id_vars, value_vars, var_name, value_name) — wide → long
- DataFrame.stack — column index → row index
- DataFrame.unstack — row index → column index
- DataFrame.crosstab(&gender, &dept) — contingency table
- DataFrame.crosstab_normalize(&gender, &dept, "all") — with normalization
- DataFrame.get_dummies(&["col1", "col2"]) — one-hot encoding
- DataFrame.xs(&IndexLabel) — cross-section selection

 {"id":"frankenpandas-tem9","title":"fd90.79: fix DISC-012 - parse_datetime_string_with_timezone_pattern coerces outliers to NaT","description":"Implement the fix for DISC-012 (refined by fd90.78). parse_datetime_string_with_timezone_pattern currently coerces opposite-pattern rows to NaT, causing parse_csv_datetime_column to bail on mixed-tz CSV columns and leave them as raw input strings. Fix: in the Naive branch, when has_tz_suffix is true, fall through to parse_tz_aware_datetime + format_aware_datetime instead of returning NaT. Same idea (with naive parser fallback) for the Aware branch when has_tz_suffix is false. The infer_mixed_timezone flag's whole point is to support mixed columns, so individual outliers should parse independently while the inferred pattern controls the column-level dtype intent. Test: FP-P2D-429 packet should go from FAIL to PASS after the fix; existing parse_dates tests still green.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-26T07:51:21.871924414Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:56:00.015040659Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T07:56:00.012408275Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","fd90","fix","review"]}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-thty","title":"[HIGH] style: no rustfmt.toml — style opinions implicit + swarm agents burn cycles on format churn","description":"Style consistency audit.

**Observation.** No `rustfmt.toml` nor `.rustfmt.toml` exists at repo root or in any crate. CI runs `cargo fmt --check` (ci.yml line ~28) which enforces rustfmt's DEFAULT style — but the workspace has not captured any style-choice decisions.

**Consequence in an active multi-agent swarm.**
- Each agent's local rustfmt may differ from CI's if they use different nightly dates (see br-1d9y about nightly pin). A nightly-to-nightly rustfmt diff can reformat hundreds of lines silently, breaking `git diff` review for the PR.
- Opinions like `max_width = 100` (default) vs 120 (often preferred for wide dataframe column lists), `imports_granularity = "Crate"` vs "Module", `group_imports = "StdExternalCrate"`, `edition = "2024"` (important for the workspace's 2024-edition choice!) — all live in rustfmt.toml and all are currently unfixed, defaulting silently.
- Today's session: my edits to fp-frame/src/lib.rs (row_multiindex field, group_keys_as_row_multiindex helper) had my rustfmt reformat the surrounding `.groupby(&groupby_refs)` from one line to three (commit f7a4e59, original lib-updater noted the reformatting as incidental). With no rustfmt.toml, this is agent-specific churn, not a shared style decision.

**Secondary: `edition = "2024"` in per-crate Cargo.toml files is NOT sufficient for rustfmt** — rustfmt.toml has its own `edition = "2024"` field that the workspace currently doesn't set. Mismatch between Cargo.toml edition and rustfmt edition produces incorrect formatting in rare cases (2024-specific syntax like the `gen` keyword).

**Severity.** HIGH — compound cost across every PR. Each swarm agent burns cycles on style churn rather than code review. CI catches the final state but doesn't prevent the churn.

**Fix shape:**

`rustfmt.toml` at repo root, conservative but explicit:
```
edition = "2024"
max_width = 100
hard_tabs = false
tab_spaces = 4
newline_style = "Unix"
use_small_heuristics = "Default"
imports_granularity = "Crate"
group_imports = "StdExternalCrate"
reorder_imports = true
reorder_modules = true
unstable_features = true    # rustfmt grew many opinions behind nightly
```

(The project is already pinned to nightly via rust-toolchain.toml, so unstable rustfmt options are available.)

Also:
1. ci.yml's `cargo fmt --check` already works but gains enforcement of the explicit opinions once rustfmt.toml lands.
2. Pre-commit hook: add `.git/hooks/hooks.d/pre-commit/10-rustfmt.sh` that runs `cargo fmt -- --check` (or `rustfmt --check` on staged .rs files) before commit. Fails fast locally, saves a CI round-trip.
3. Document the style choice in CONTRIBUTING.md (sibling br-frankenpandas-6d5s).

Blast radius: 1 new file (~15 lines), 1 new pre-commit hook (~20 lines), possibly a one-time reformatting commit after rustfmt.toml lands (large diff but mechanical). Recommend batching the reformat into a single commit tagged `chore: apply rustfmt.toml opinions` that reviewers can fast-scroll past.

Related:
- br-6d5s (CONTRIBUTING.md) — style notes live here.
- br-1d9y (nightly date pin) — rustfmt version implicitly pinned by the nightly pin, reducing reformat drift.
- br-iy8u (pre-commit doctrine) — adding a rustfmt hook extends the existing pre-commit discipline.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:39:17.551317919Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:28:26.542652618Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T16:28:26.540109900Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review","rustfmt","style","swarm"]}

@@ -1825,3 +1825,93 @@ fn readme_dataframe_constructors_compiles_and_runs() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::
     assert_eq!(df_mi.index().len(), 2);
     Ok(())
 }
+
+/// README "Reshaping" section (lines 498-524).
+///
+/// Locks in 7 reshaping APIs that previously had no integration coverage
+/// (pivot_table is already exercised by readme_pivot_tables):
+///
+/// - DataFrame.melt (wide → long)
+/// - DataFrame.stack / unstack (column index ↔ row index round-trip)
+/// - DataFrame.crosstab + crosstab_normalize (contingency tables)
+/// - DataFrame.get_dummies (one-hot encoding)
+/// - DataFrame.xs (cross-section by IndexLabel)
+///
+/// Tracks fd90.193 (br-frankenpandas-tc3g2).
+#[test]
+fn readme_reshaping_compiles_and_runs() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
+    // melt — wide → long with id_vars + value_vars.
+    let wide = read_csv_str("id,q1,q2,q3\n1,10,20,30\n2,40,50,60")?;
+    let melted = wide.melt(&["id"], &["q1", "q2", "q3"], Some("quarter"), Some("sales"))?;
+    assert!(
+        melted
+            .column_names()
+            .iter()
+            .any(|n| n.as_str() == "quarter")
+    );
+    assert!(
+        melted
+            .column_names()
+            .iter()
+            .any(|n| n.as_str() == "sales")
+    );
+
+    // stack / unstack — round-trip exercise on a numeric DataFrame.
+    let df = read_csv_str("a,b\n1,2\n3,4")?;
+    let stacked = df.stack()?;
+    assert!(stacked.index().len() >= 2);
+    let _unstacked = stacked.unstack()?;
+
+    // crosstab — contingency table from two categorical Series.
+    let labels: Vec<IndexLabel> = (0..6i64).map(IndexLabel::Int64).collect();
+    let gender = Series::from_values(
+        "gender",
+        labels.clone(),
+        vec![
+            "M".into(),
+            "F".into(),
+            "M".into(),
+            "F".into(),
+            "M".into(),
+            "F".into(),
+        ],
+    )?;
+    let dept = Series::from_values(
+        "department",
+        labels,
+        vec![
+            "eng".into(),
+            "eng".into(),
+            "sales".into(),
+            "sales".into(),
+            "ops".into(),
+            "ops".into(),
+        ],
+    )?;
+    let ct = DataFrame::crosstab(&gender, &dept)?;
+    assert_eq!(ct.column_names().len(), 3); // 3 unique departments
+    assert_eq!(ct.index().len(), 2); // 2 unique genders
+
+    // crosstab_normalize — divide by grand total.
+    let ct_norm = DataFrame::crosstab_normalize(&gender, &dept, "all")?;
+    assert_eq!(ct_norm.column_names().len(), 3);
+
+    // get_dummies — one-hot encoding on string-typed columns.
+    let cat_df = read_csv_str("color,size,price\nred,S,10\nblue,M,20\nred,L,30")?;
+    let dummies = cat_df.get_dummies(&["color", "size"])?;
+    // Each unique category becomes a dummy column; "price" is preserved.
+    assert!(
+        dummies
+            .column_names()
+            .iter()
+            .any(|n| n.as_str() == "price")
+    );
+    assert!(dummies.column_names().len() > 1);
+
+    // xs — cross-section by IndexLabel (uses From<&str> for IndexLabel).
+    let dated_df = read_csv_str("date,price\n2024-01-15,100\n2024-01-16,105\n2024-01-17,110")?
+        .set_index("date", true)?;
+    let row = dated_df.xs(&"2024-01-15".into())?;
+    assert_eq!(row.index().len(), 1);
+    Ok(())
+}
